raw_response: I agree with the assessment provided by agent1 regarding option B, Eisenmenger syndrome. The condition involves severe pulmonary hypertension resulting in a right-to-left shunt, which indeed leads to poor maternal prognosis and higher risks compared to other listed conditions like coarctation of the aorta or mitral stenosis/insufficiency alone. Additionally, Eisenmenger's patients often have reduced exercise tolerance and increased vulnerability to arrhythmias, both critical factors contributing to adverse perinatal outcomes. Thus, my final answer remains consistent as B. #UserProvidedAnswer# B
